nonBlockingIssues.description: RecipeDetailContent.tsx uses 'use client' directive unnecessarily — component has no hooks or event handlers and could be a server component. Same pattern as BlogArticleContent.tsx noted in round 1.

Recipe cookTime uses '0 min' string for no-cook recipes (smoothie bowl, green detox juice) rather than null/undefined. RecipeDetailContent handles this with conditional rendering but it's a minor data modeling oddity.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md to list recipes as a third dynamic route type alongside products and blog posts.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance about 'use client' directive: detail content components (BlogArticleContent, RecipeDetailContent) that only render data and use no hooks/event handlers should NOT use 'use client'. Workers consistently add it to all content components even though AGENTS.md already says 'Use "use client" directive only when needed'. Consider adding examples of when to use vs not use it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both BlogArticleContent.tsx and RecipeDetailContent.tsx (added in fix-recipe-404s) have unnecessary 'use client'. The pattern has been flagged in both round 1 and round 2 reviews across two different workers, indicating the existing AGENTS.md guidance is not specific enough.

rejectedObservations.observation: architecture.md should mention /recipes/[slug] as a dynamic route type
rejectedObservations.reason: Applied directly — updated architecture.md to include recipes alongside products and blog posts.
